name: sales
instructions:
["There are two employees, Susan and Mike, in the sales department of a paper company’s local branch. Every day, both employees try to sell as many reams of paper as possible. The local branch must sell over 167 reams of paper in order to make a profit for their company. So, if Susan and Mike together sell more than 167 reams of paper, then their branch will make a profit for that day.

Based on the average amount of paper Susan and Mike each sell per day, the branch rarely makes a profit.","We will show you how many reams of paper each of the two employees sold on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the branch made a profit.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how much paper is sold by each employee on average."]

name: sales
instructions:
["There are two employees, Susan and Mike, in the sales department of a paper company’s local branch. Every day, both employees try to sell as many reams of paper as possible. The local branch must sell over 167 reams of paper in order to make a profit for their company. So, if Susan and Mike together sell more than 167 reams of paper, then their branch will make a profit for that day.

Based on the average amount of paper Susan and Mike each sell per day, the branch rarely makes a profit.","We will show you how many reams of paper each of the two employees sold on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the branch made a profit.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how much paper is sold by each employee on average."]

The local branch must sell over 167 reams of paper in order to make a profit for their company. So, if Susan and Mike together sell more than 167 reams of paper, then their branch will make a profit for that day.

Based on the average amount of paper Susan and Mike each sell per day, the branch rarely makes a profit.","We will show you how many reams of paper each of the two employees sold on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the branch made a profit.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how much paper is sold by each employee on average."]

name: sales
instructions:
["There are two employees, Susan and Mike, in the sales department of a paper company’s local branch. Every day, both employees try to sell as many reams of paper as possible. The local branch must sell over 167 reams of paper in order to make a profit for their company. So, if Susan and Mike together sell more than 167 reams of paper, then their branch will make a profit for that day.

Based on the average amount of paper Susan and Mike each sell per day, the branch rarely makes a profit.","We will show you how many reams of paper each of the two employees sold on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the branch made a profit.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how much paper is sold by each employee on average."]

["There are two employees, Susan and Mike, in the sales department of a paper company’s local branch. Every day, both employees try to sell as many reams of paper as possible. The local branch must sell over 167 reams of paper in order to make a profit for their company. So, if Susan and Mike together sell more than 167 reams of paper, then their branch will make a profit for that day.

Based on the average amount of paper Susan and Mike each sell per day, the branch rarely makes a profit.","We will show you how many reams of paper each of the two employees sold on 40 days in total, separated into 4 blocks of 10 individual days. For each day, you will be asked whether the branch made a profit.

At the end of each block, you will be asked how much paper is sold by each employee on average."]
